Gabriel Bonnin
Bio Projects Publications

Projects

Research projects spanning psychotherapy effectiveness, diagnostic assessment, and AI/NLP applications in clinical psychology.

Current

2026 Current

META – Mental Health Evaluation through Text Analysis

Applies state-of-the-art large language models to open-ended intake data from a German outpatient psychotherapy clinic. Uses AI/NLP to measure psychological constructs through natural language, complementing traditional assessment methods. Focuses on real-world clinical settings rather than social media data.

Natural Language ProcessingMental HealthAssessmentLLM
2025 Current

SAVER – AI-Driven Speech Analysis in Psychotherapy

Multi-center database project across DZPG sites building a unique dataset of psychotherapy sessions. Combines audio recordings with diagnostic interviews and self-report measures to study relationships between language features and clinical outcomes.

PsychotherapyAISpeech AnalysisMachine LearningMental Health
2026 Current

POSIDONIA – Personalised Modular Psychotherapy for Treatment-Resistant Depression

Sequential Bayesian randomized parallel-group trial within DZPG evaluating personalised modular psychotherapy vs. Behavioral Activation for treatment-resistant depression. Aims to modify clinical guidelines and establish adaptive trial infrastructure.

PsychotherapyDepressionPersonalisationRCTDZPG

Previous

2023

BO36 – Long-term Effectiveness and Transgenerational Transmission of Psychotherapy

Examines the sustainability of psychotherapy effects over more than a decade in children, adolescents, adults, and families. Follows cohorts from RCTs and routine care settings with annual tracking, with a central focus on intergenerational dynamics.

PsychotherapyLong-term EffectsSustainabilityTransgenerational
2020

DISKO – Learning Diagnostic Skills Online

Blended-learning programs for teaching structured diagnostic methods using DIPS-OA and Kinder-DIPS-OA. Tested in a multi-center cluster-randomized trial with 350 students, showing superior outcomes vs. traditional teaching formats.

Diagnostic InterviewsMental DisordersTherapist Traininge-Learning
2023

PT-INF – DZPG Psychotherapy Research Infrastructure

One of twelve cross-site research infrastructures within DZPG. A digital long-term data platform for harmonized data collection from routine psychotherapeutic care, coordinating data collection across multiple German mental health centers.

PsychotherapyPractice Research NetworkRoutine Outcome MonitoringDZPG
